title: Four nuclear reactors hit a big milestone in the US
gist: Four US microreactor startups (Antares Nuclear, Valar Atomics, Deployable Energy, Aalo Atomics) achieved zero-power criticality by July 4, 2026, beating a Trump-administration goal of three under the DOE Reactor Pilot Program - but criticality is far from grid-ready power, timelines are aggressive, and NRC approval plus expert skepticism remain the gating risks.

insights.claim: Four US microreactor companies achieved criticality by July 4, 2026, exceeding a Trump-administration goal of three new microreactors sustaining a chain reaction by the nation's 250th birthday.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: A positive signal for the emerging-nuclear sector's technical execution, but a symbolic/political deadline that markets should not confuse with commercial readiness.
insights.locator: Last year the Trump administration set a goal

insights.claim: Antares Nuclear reached criticality first in June in its Mark-0 test reactor, followed by Valar Atomics, Deployable Energy, and Aalo Atomics, with Aalo hitting the mark in the early hours of July 4.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: Names the four private frontrunners in US microreactors - a watchlist for private/eventual public exposure to advanced nuclear.

insights.claim: The DOE Reactor Pilot Program selected 11 reactor projects in August, offering them land and national-lab support to fast-track prototype microreactors.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: Federal land and lab support is a de-risking subsidy for the sector; the 11-project pipeline defines the near-term competitive field beyond the four that hit criticality.
insights.locator: The Reactor Pilot Program essentially opened a special door

insights.claim: These reactors are microreactors, tens or even hundreds of times smaller than the large light-water reactors that dominate the grid today.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: Scale context: microreactors target niche/distributed loads (e.g. data centers), not baseload grid replacement, bounding their near-term market size.

insights.claim: All four reactors achieved only zero-power criticality - a test of starting a chain reaction with no meaningful power output - which is very different from running a reactor that produces electricity.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: The key reality check: headlines overstate progress; producing grid electricity requires added systems like cooling and real fuel/design engineering.
insights.locator: All these reactors reached what’s called zero-power criticality

insights.claim: A zero-power-criticality test can be achieved without making real engineering progress on fuel or design, according to former assistant secretary for nuclear energy Kathryn Huff.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: An authoritative caveat that undercuts the milestone's investment significance - use it to discount startup progress claims.
insights.quote: A zero-power-criticality test can be achieved without making real engineering progress on fuel or design,

insights.claim: To make electricity these companies will need to add significant equipment such as cooling systems to transfer heat out of the reactor core, posing potentially large technical challenges.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: The remaining engineering (thermal management, fuel) is where cost overruns and delays historically hit nuclear - the real risk after the milestone.
insights.locator: In some cases they’ll need to add significant equipment

insights.claim: Aalo Atomics has begun work on a second reactor and plans to produce 10 megawatts of electricity to power an on-site data center in 2027, while Deployable Energy plans to deploy commercial reactors by 2028.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: Data-center power is the explicit near-term customer for microreactors; these 2027-2028 milestones are the dates to track for the AI-power-demand nuclear thesis.
insights.locator: Aalo says it’s already begun work

insights.claim: The author cautions that startup timelines in nuclear deserve skepticism because these are complex machines and companies often hit problems outside their control such as regulatory challenges.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: Discount aggressive 2027-2028 deployment claims; nuclear's history of blown deadlines and budgets argues for caution on timing-dependent bets.
insights.quote: I tend to take timelines from startups, especially in nuclear, with a grain of salt.
insights.locator: The companies are projecting aggressive timelines

insights.claim: The four leading microreactor companies are very young: Valar, Antares, and Aalo were founded in 2023 and Deployable started in 2025.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: Reaching criticality in ~2-3 years from founding is genuinely fast for nuclear, but youth means unproven ability to scale to commercial operation.
insights.locator: Valar, Antares, and Aalo were all founded in 2023

insights.claim: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission's historically slow approval process is a gating risk, and while it proposed a new microreactor framework to speed approvals, some experts worry the agency under Trump is loosening rules too much.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: Regulatory pace is the binary swing factor for commercialization timing; a safety-related setback under looser rules could stall the whole sector.
insights.locator: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission is in charge

insights.claim: The think tank Third Way argues the federal microreactor focus is an 'unhelpful diversion' from meaningfully increasing nuclear capacity and that artificially accelerating timelines is a short-term solution, not a long-term fix.
insights.speaker: Casey Crownhart
insights.so_what: Even pro-nuclear voices see the microreactor push as a distraction from grid-scale capacity - a bear case on where public/policy resources should flow.
insights.quote: Artificially accelerating project timelines is a short-term solution, not a long-term fix,
